title: can angels be poisoned
wikitext:
[]: can angels be poisoned?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Nov 23, 2009 1:53 pm can they. i was just thinking, what if kaworu could have been poisoned. then i got to thinking, if he could be, could the other angels be poisoned? all NERV would have had to do was leave a very sharp poison covered spike in front of them. would have saved money. they would only have needed the EVAs for a few angels. is there even an answer to this? anybody know. lets discuss the implications of this.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Nov 23, 2009 1:57 pm It would have to be an Anti-AT Field spike...

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:01 pm master_lloyd wrote: It would have to be an Anti-AT Field spike...

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:06 pm shinjidoggysasuka wrote: not really, remember, sachiel stepped on an N2 mine which only did collateral damage without noticing. it only did a little surface damage, but the point is, it didn't notice, so it could have just stepped on a spike, got pissed, and 15 minutes later dropped dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:08 pm Sachiel didn't step on an N2 mine. The military dropped it on him. Anyway it's possible than an angel can get poisoned but not likely to achieve.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:13 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Sachiel didn't step on an N2 mine. The military dropped it on him. Anyway it's possible than an angel can get poisoned but not likely to achieve.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:14 pm Because Anno didnt want them too.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:22 pm shinjidoggysasuka wrote: well than it makes you think, why didn't NERV try that a few times. or tip the lance of longinus in poison? or even the progressive knife?

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:24 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: shinjidoggysasuka wrote: well than it makes you think, why didn't NERV try that a few times. or tip the lance of longinus in poison? or even the progressive knife?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:26 pm If it did work, then yes but very unlikely.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:31 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: If it did work, then yes but very unlikely.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:41 pm Each angel is different. It's unlikely them same poison would work for each and would probably take to long to develop even if it could. It also wouldn't work for all angels like for Gaghiel , Israfael , Sandalphon , Sahaquiel , Ireul , Leliel , Zuruel , Arael , and Armisael for certain reasons. Pretty much most of the angels during the second half of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:43 pm So in other words nearly ALL of them?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:55 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: So in other words nearly ALL of them?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Nov 23, 2009 2:58 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: The only angel you probably have a shot at would probably be Matarael. It's the weakest angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Mon Nov 23, 2009 3:18 pm Instead of injecting it with a spike, why not a poisonous gas. We do know that Sachiel could breath, but with the other Angels it is unknown as to which others breath air (gassing Ramiel to death is very unlikely to work). The problem with poison gas is because most Angels are very large, you need a lot of it depending on how toxic. With so much toxic gas, it needs to be somewhere far away from people and water sources. Then again dumping poison in liquid form into orifices could work if we know it will affect the target Angel's physiology. One could trick Gaghiel into eating canisters of poison, pour it into the eye sockets of the masks of Angels with bony masks, or just keep dumping it all over them if things get desperate. The problem with this is that it could easily contaminate water and food sources. NERV probably wouldn't take that big of a rist unless things got really desperate. The biggest problem of all is that Angels aren't made up of the same matter we are, and poisons made from regular matter probably wouldn't work at all. Maybe one would create a poison made from PWM. master_lloyd wrote: Why am I now thinking of NERV using over sized mouse-traps to kill the angels?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Nov 23, 2009 3:42 pm Ireul was almost poisoned and killed by ozone in episode #13, so I suppose it's at least a possibility. That said, I tend to think that more robust PWM bodies wouldn't be susceptible to that sort of thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon Nov 23, 2009 3:51 pm Actually I think this is pretty unlikely to work, unless an angels core is destroyed they pretty much won't die. Even if the poison does damage it'll just get healed. But even if it does work here's the problem, no one knows how the angels body works, it would be trial and error testing to see what poisons may or may npot work, unless they get it on the first try the angels would wise up to any spike traps that might get lain around after getting a couple spikes in the foot.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Tue Nov 24, 2009 11:40 pm I think a virus would be a better way of dealing with them than poison. Angels are "human," after all.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Nov 24, 2009 11:46 pm "but they don't have human form" or something similar to that.
